So some of you guys may see in the group that some people do 20 pounds, 20 and 5 for a very long time, and then I think we did 25 and 5 for the last year or two of my gyms. And what's interesting is that we've had no difference. I think it goes to show you that a lot of people just will do whatever it takes to hit the goal. And those types of people will hit the goal whatever it is. So just a fun one for you. So I recommend doing 25 pounds for people who have a BMI of 28 or over, which means they are overweight on the edge of being more than obese by the BMI tracking. And if someone has a normal BMI, 7, 26, 25, 34, any of those, body fat percentage is the goal. So essentially we're doing recomposition for them so they're gaining more muscle and losing more fat, but their weight won't change. They do 10% forever. So if someone comes in to their 150, 300, they lose 30. And that is the goal. So it kind of, but it is 100% weight loss for everyone in that case. It does eliminate the issues with the omron, which I think is going to be the next question I answer. The omron handheld means you can use an in-body or fit 3D, whatever one you want. We use an omron because it was omron accuracy. I'll count this and I'll answer the other one. So accurate, meaning under the same conditions, if you measure it multiple times, the numbers won't be very off. They'll be more or less pretty close together. The issue is just accuracy, which means that if someone is changing the conditions, then the numbers will waver more. So the biggest thing that influences that is hydration levels and how full of glycogen their muscles are. So if someone just carved up their actual body fat percentage on the omron will go down, which is why if someone is trying that they're more likely to hit their body fat percentage goal, which is why we tell people we're going to change things up on you so that we can help you hit the goal. And it is too good to be true. So sometimes it's nice to have a little bit of negative. So overweight person, what do you do? Super overweight person. The easiest thing to with these people is like, you just want them to move. So like to a facility and like getting on the ground and standing back up is already going to be like a full fledged workout. Some really, really overweight people can't even get on the ground. So in those situations, just have them do simple movements. I think wall says that they can do in the meantime as they continue to lose the weight. If they feel frustrated, just tell them, listen, like you're here at the gym and I'm here to help you out. I want you to walk around the facility for 30 minutes come in like so they can do the warm up walk around and then do the cool down. Like there's a million ways to spend it. But the point is, is like if you need them to get more activity and walking is the best thing for a super overweight person and for them, it's going to be just the coaching and the accountability to make sure that they follow through stuff.
